videos.title: WSO Water Treatment Grades 3 & 4: Ozone & UV, Ch. 11

transcript.fullText: ozone that's used for disinfecting water is similar to bo-zone created in nature when lightning electrifies oxygen in the atmosphere an electrical charge supplies energy to bind the third oxygen atom during ozone production for water treatment the source oxygen is energized by a high-voltage spark and the ozone is applied to water however ozone is an unstable gas and under normal circumstances it quickly returns to oxygen therefore when ozone is used as a disinfectant it can only be generated on site it can't be stored or shipped once ozone production and contact has been completed off gases remain that need to be removed the next alternative disinfection will focus on is UV UV has been used in Europe for decades in the United States UV has been used for a number of years at wastewater treatment facilities and in small drinking water applications and systems in the 1990s researchers discovered that UV was a very effective disinfecting tool capable of in activating Cryptosporidium and Giardia the UV lamps inside the stainless steel tube are housed in a quartz sleeve it's important these sleeves are monitored and cleaned periodically because deposits or stains from debris and minerals of passing water can limit the penetration of the UV light UV lights don't burn out but rather diminish in intensity and lose their effectiveness and must be replaced some plants replace their lamps yearly as a precaution another popular characteristic of UV treatment is that it doesn't create any regulated disinfection by-products however that must be balanced with the fact that UV also leaves no residual in the final product to let operators know it has done its job and will protect the water throughout the distribution system ozone and UV disinfection will continue to be valuable tools in the utility operators quest to treat water with the most efficient and effective technology available

videos.title: What Are The Benefits Of Ozonated Water? - The Pro Dentist

transcript.fullText: What are the benefits of ozonated water? Have you ever wondered how ozonated water could impact your dental health? Ozonated water is simply water that has been infused with ozone, a form of oxygen that is highly reactive. In the field of dentistry, this unique water has been gaining attention for its potential benefits. One major advantage of ozonated water is its strong antimicrobial properties. It can effectively eliminate bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which is crucial for reducing the risk of infections in the mouth. This makes it a valuable tool for promoting a cleaner oral environment. Another benefit of ozonated water is its ability to enhance oxygen metabolism in tissues. This can stimulate healing and repair, which is particularly important for any wounds or infections in the mouth. In practical terms, ozonated water can help control and prevent dental diseases by targeting harmful pathogens without relying on chemicals or traditional antibiotics. This aspect makes it appealing for those who prefer more natural approaches to dental care. When it comes to preventing tooth decay, ozonated water can play a significant role. Regular use may disrupt the growth of cavity causing bacteria and it can even halt the progression of early tooth decay. This means that patients might reduce their need for fillings, which is a win-win situation for both dental health and finances. For those dealing with gum diseases, ozonated water has shown promise in managing these conditions. It can reduce bacterial loads in gum pockets, helping to promote healing and reduce inflammation. Healthier gums contribute to overall oral hygiene, which is essential for maintaining a healthy mouth. In terms of practical applications, ozonated water is often used in periodontal therapy. It can irrigate periodontal pockets, effectively reducing infections and promoting healthier gum tissue. During root canal treatments, ozone therapy can disinfect the root canal system, minimizing the risk of infection. Additionally, ozonated water can enhance the effectiveness of deep cleaning and scaling procedures, leading to faster healing and improved outcomes. In summary, ozonated water offers several benefits in dentistry, including its antimicrobial action, improved wound healing, and its role in preventing dental diseases. However, it is important to use ozonated water under the guidance of dental professionals to ensure safe and effective treatment. Always consult with your dentist to see how ozonated water might fit into your oral health routine.

videos.title: [ Ozonation ] - Learn How Ozonation Process Works

transcript.fullText: ozonation ozone is a triatomic form of oxygen which is composed of three oxygen atoms when UV light hits oxygen gas it breaks it down to two oxygen atoms then when an oxygen atom meets oxygen gas it forms Ozone gas because ozone is unstable and cannot be stored successfully it must be generated at the point of application by ozone generator the process is carried out by an electric discharge field as in the CD type ozone generators or by ultraviolet radiations as in UV type ozone generators in the ozone generator oxygen molecules that is O2 are split into individual oxygen atoms in this state oxygen is very unstable so it joins with other oxygen molecules to form O3 which is Ozone the raw water is then passed through a Venturi injector which creates a vacuum and pulls the Ozone gas into the water is then bubbled up through the water being treated after injection the ozone must have time to do its work this time is known as contact Time contact time is the time ozone has to oxidize and disinfect or the time the water is allowed to hold the disinfectant for ozone contact time is measured from the point of injection to the filter the contact time needed varies with the matter to be oxidized since the ozone will react with metals to create insoluble metal oxides post filteration is required the water is spermed by the pressure tanks to the automatic filteration system the post filteration process is performed by an automatic filteration system after the water gets post filtered then it is fit for the household purposes and clean filtered water is supplied to houses

videos.title: Ozone Therapy With Dr. Kelly Misch

transcript.fullText: [Music] my name is kelly mish and i'm a periodontist at michigan implants and periodontics and today we're talking about ozone here at mip we are very excited to be the leaders in a lot of different periodontal procedures and we're using the latest techniques and one of my favorite is is having ozone because i think it's made a huge difference in our practice and in my patients success rates and i'm really excited about that ozone is something you've probably heard of ozone being in the atmosphere you know after a lightning storm you can actually smell it it has a really distinct smell to it it's basically three molecules of oxygen at the office we use ozone for treating for multiple different things one thing we use it for is treating infections so someone that has a mouth infection or tooth infection we use it after we take the tooth out to disinfect the site or assist we use it for treating herpes complex cold sores that you can get in your lips around your nose or in your mouth it can be really painful ulcers that can last up to two weeks and ozone therapy can make that last for only up to like one or two days so it's really really a great product for for patients that have had these chronic things we also use it for treating cavities so someone that may have a small cavity and they're not ready to have a filling in it we could treat it with ozone gas and it may prolong that time for them to get a filling or they may never have to have that we use it for two sensitivity so someone that has something called recession where their roots are showing we put ozone gas on it and it helps with with cold sensitivity and sensitivity to air that a lot of patients complain about you may have patients that take like sensitive toothpaste and so this is something that they may not have to use that anymore we also use it for disorders of diseases in the mouth that they have a lot of pain and ulcers and autoimmune issues and so it can help reduce that with it we use it you know for a multitude of different things because it's antibacterial we also have it in an oil form so this is ozone oil and it can be in hemp seed flaxseed olive oil avocado oil coconut oil you know anything you can think of and so the ozone you keep this in the refrigerator too but this is really good if you get like a cold sore or a bug bite and it takes the sting away it takes burns away and it makes it heal really really quickly so i just wanted to make other people aware of what ozone is and in dentistry what the uses are and how we use it at our office my partners and i want the best for our patients we have the best technology and we want our patients to be safe and ozone is something that has absolutely no contraindications and we're really excited about videos.title: Can an Ozone Generator Kill House Plants?

transcript.fullText: everyone from cannabis farmers to the national park service itself claims that ozone can be harmful to plants i want to find out for myself how true this really is to do this i started by building a simple plywood box with a glass front i then cut a hole in one far end where i mounted an ozone generator by doing this all the air that gets sucked into the box has to go through the ozone generator so we can get the highest concentration of ozone possible there's a slit at the top of the glass so that the ozone can circulate out as it's depleted and then fresh air can be sucked in and converted ozone i got a hold of a little house plant just a common maiden hair fern put it in the box and fired up the ozone generator i let this run all night long and i did it overnight so that excess heat wouldn't be an issue and i came back the next morning after being in there for about 13 hours and checked it out to see what happened not surprisingly the fern seemed a little dried out the leaves were just a little crispy some of them were slightly discolored and the soil was a little less damp than it was the night before was that the ozone or was that just the fan from the ozone generator if i had a fan blowing in my face for 13 hours i'd probably feel a little dried out too so i took our little fern inside and gave it the best care i knew how i gave it good sunlight but made sure to keep it out of direct sunlight i kept it watered but not saturated i just took care of it like i would want to be treated if i were a fern the six week checkup revealed more dead dry leaves i would later learn that these maiden hair ferns just don't put up with much abuse so is all this damage caused by ozone or by the fan and the touching and handling and all the other abuse that i've put it through not knowing for sure i decided to try a different plant meet the golden pothos if you've ever seen a pothos plant it was probably sitting on top of a bookshelf or somewhere else tall where it's long vines could hang down and grow for years on end so i fired up the ozone chamber and threw the pothos plant inside i let it run all night long into the afternoon or the next day for a total of about 17 hours the damage was pretty evident a few leaves had some brown spots on them kind of some discoloration one was pretty well yellowed out and there were some definite defects that were not there the previous night then just as i did with the fern i took the pothos back inside and gave it tender loving care for another four weeks and here we are at the four week check-in and i think it's stabilized pretty well the leaves that were yellowed out and browned out a little have pretty much completely died off but everything else seems to come back green and uh overall in pretty good shape so am i saying that my little backyard experiment invalidates decades of scientific research no of course not but it's still been a fun demonstration in just how resilient plants can be if given a proper environment to heal and some time to bounce back so now that i have this ozone chamber it'd really be a shame not to use it so let me know in the comments tell me what you want to see put to the test against ozone gas and hopefully you can draw this conclusion for yourself bottom line if you're going to run an ozone generator in a room and you have a plant that you care about even a little bit just take the plant out of the room
